meaning: adj.怪诞的,可怕的,不安的,奇异的
examples: He had the eerie sensation of being watched.
As I entered the hallway which led to my room that eerie feeling came over me.
There is something undeniably soulful about the eerie whine of the highland bagpipes.
An optical laser reads the transparency and creates a wobbly, out-of-tune, almost eerie-sounding keyboard effect.
In the cracks between the stones, there is here and there an eerie, electric green glow.
